title: kyllinga nervosa subsp. flava
intro: Kyllinga nervosa subsp. flava, also known as yellow Kyllinga, is a type of grassy plant native to Australia and parts of Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant has been used to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. In traditional medicine, Kyllinga nervosa subsp. flava is often prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves in hot water, and it can also be made into a poultice or ointment for topical application.
